what_it_does: A bash script (Gate D handle) that mints the throwaway design_client JWT and reads the 'design' channel history through the connector Edge Function, printing each post's from_session, sender_kind, and text to prove the CD client and the fork's company session see the same rows in their shared channel.
observations: Mints a token via password grant for design-test@company.local using the inline supabase-demo ANON key.
Exits FAIL if no token is returned.
Calls the channels tool with op:history on channel 'design' via JSON-RPC against the Edge Function at 127.0.0.1:8773/mcp.
Parses the JSON-RPC result text, and if it's a JSON array prints from_session | sender_kind | first 60 chars of text per row.
Header comments state the expectation: BOTH sides' posts (CD claude-design and the fork's company session) should appear.
Designed to run AFTER the fork posts to 'design' (from_session != claude-design).
standouts: Goal is to prove two-way participation: same row visible to both principals via RLS in the shared channel.
Single-purpose companion to gate D in the broader gate suite.
conventions: Same inline-curl + python3 JSON parsing idiom as the companion .cd-drive.sh.
Same throwaway-cred / gitignored self-documentation header convention.
concerns: Embeds the demo ANON JWT and test password in plaintext (local/demo, but in-repo).
set -u only; a malformed history response falls back to printing raw text but won't fail the script.
notes: A focused verification handle proving the design_client can read the shared 'design' channel history and sees the fork's company-side posts (same data, two-way).
questions: What is sender_kind's vocabulary, and how does it distinguish CD vs company-session posts?
Does the broader gate suite have separate scripts for the other gates, or is this the only standalone gate-D handle?
purpose_vs_actual: Stated purpose (Gate D — CD reads design channel history to prove shared-row two-way participation) matches what the script does.
intention_signals: Trying to demonstrate that a granted shared channel is genuinely shared — the same persisted rows are visible to both the CD client and the fork's company session via RLS.
contribution: Contributes the gate-D verification: an executable read-side proof of shared-channel two-way participation through the connector.
summary_for_embedding: A bash script that mints a design_client JWT and reads the shared 'design' channel history via the Edge Function to prove both CD and company-session posts appear.
intention_for_embedding: To prove two-way shared-channel participation by showing the design_client sees the fork's posts in the same channel under RLS.
